rulings.Case Title: JAMES FARRIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 07, 2014 in Department 503, Line 6. Plaintiff's motion for order granting leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL HAYNES et al VS. BASF CORPORATION INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Carol Haynes, Earl Haynes Motion And Application For Leave To Amend The Second Amended Complaint To Reflect Plaintiff'S Death In The Alternative An Order Shortening Time To Hear Plaintiffs' Motion For Leave To Amend (Advanced From 05/08/14 To 05/07/14 Per Court Order Of 05/01/14)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 07, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to amend the first amended complaint to reflect plaintiff's death is granted. Earl Haynes is substituted as successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff Carol Haynes. Plaintiffs are granted leave to file a second amended complaint. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA PUCHOSIC, AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO AND et al VS. CLEAVER-BROOKS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Abate Entire Action Pending Joinder Of All Necessary Parties
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 07, 2014 in Department 503, Line 7. Defendant E.F. Brady Company, Inc.'s motion to abate entire action pending joinder of all necessary parties is granted. Plaintiffs are compelled to properly join David Puchosic. This action is stayed until then. If and when plaintiffs serve David Puchosic with the summons and complaint, plaintiffs may move the court to vacate the stay. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
